notes.note: The two Spanish provinces, where Pompey's legates Afranius and Petreius faced Caesar, were the strategic pivot of the war in spring 49 BC; Cicero repeatedly makes his own decision to leave Italy contingent on the outcome there.

notes.anchor: I wish you had taken on the steering of the lad yourself
notes.note: Cicero's nephew, young Quintus, was a source of family anxiety throughout these months; his father (Cicero's brother Quintus) is reproached here as too indulgent to discipline the boy.

notes.anchor: Pompey is setting out for Gaul by way of Illyricum
notes.note: A false rumour: Pompey had in fact crossed to Greece, not toward Gaul. Cicero's uncertainty about Pompey's route shaped his own dithering over where and how to follow.
